claim: Hand B tokens beginning with qo-k- form a morphological class with shared biological-section concentration. The specificity of qo-k- (vs qo-non-k-) is tested by comparing aggregate biological-concentration of the two classes. If qo-k- concentrates but qo-non-k- does not, 'k' after qo- does semantic/content work; if both concentrate, qo- alone is the biological marker; if neither concentrates in aggregate, the four-of-six qo-k- pattern in top-20 marker concentration is coincidental.
evidence_for: H-BV-HAND-B-MARKER-CONCENTRATION-01 found 4 of 6 biological-concentrated markers share the qo-k- prefix: qokain (d=1.82), qokedy (d=1.19), qokain, qokedy. Plus chedy and shedy as biological markers without the qo-k- prefix. The qo-k- cluster is suggestive but needs systematic testing.
Hand B has 184 distinct qo-k- tokens (total frequency 2532) and 360 distinct qo-non-k- tokens (total 1549). Both classes are large enough for robust statistics when aggregated per folio.

Testing both classes against biological concentration provides the comparison needed to isolate whether k specifically is doing content work (expected: qo-k- concentrates, qo-non-k- doesn't) or whether qo- alone is a marker (expected: both concentrate similarly).

A confirmed morphological-class finding would be concrete evidence of sub-morpheme structure in Voynichese: qo-k- would function as a productive biological-context morphological unit, not just a coincidental spelling pattern.
evidence_against: Voynichese morphology is inferred structurally by Brain-V, not known externally. qo- and k- may or may not be genuine morphological units — treating qo-k- as one may be a projection of familiar linguistic structure onto data that doesn't work that way.
Both classes contain a large tail of low-frequency tokens (184 distinct qo-k- forms, 360 distinct qo-non-k-). Rare tokens contribute to aggregate rates and may dilute or amplify signal irregularly.

If Hand A also has substantial qo-k- tokens but lacks biological section at sample scale, the Hand A comparison would be uninformative. This test is Hand B-specific; extension to Hand A may not be tractable.

Per H-BV-HAND-B-MARKER-CONCENTRATION-01 finding, 'ar' and 'aiin' are biological-AVOIDED (negative d). These are qo-non-k- adjacent tokens (qo+ar-like endings). If qo-non-k- class inherits this avoidance, the test may show qo-non-k- in negative direction while qo-k- in positive direction — a double-signed contrast.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  DATA: Hand B folios with >= 30 tokens from voynich-corpus.json. Same filter as prior tests.

  TOKEN CLASSES (LOCKED):
    Class A (qo-k-): all Hand B tokens beginning with the 3-character substring 'qok'. Includes qokain, qokedy, qokeedy, qokeey, qokaiin, qokal, qokar, qoky, qokey, and all others starting 'qok'.
    Class B (qo-non-k-): all Hand B tokens beginning with 'qo' but NOT 'qok'. Includes qol, qotedy, qoteedy, qotaiin, qotar, qoty, qotain, qotal, qo, and all others.
    Class C (control, neither): all other tokens.

  METRIC: per-folio aggregate rate = sum of (occurrences of any class-member token) / total_tokens_in_folio.

  PROCEDURE:
    For each Hand B folio:
      count_qok = sum over tokens in folio of (1 if token starts with 'qok' else 0)
      count_qo_non_k = sum over tokens of (1 if token starts with 'qo' AND NOT 'qok' else 0)
      rate_qok = count_qok / total_tokens
      rate_qo_non_k = count_qo_non_k / total_tokens

    Split folios into biological (n=19) vs non-biological (n=63).
    For each class (qok, qo-non-k), run Welch's t-test on rate_biological vs rate_non_biological.
    Compute Cohen's d for each class.
    Record: t, p, d, mean_in, mean_out per class.

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED):
    CONFIRMED (qo-k is morphologically semantic): d_qok >= 0.8 AND p_qok < 0.001 AND (d_qo_non_k < 0.5 OR p_qo_non_k >= 0.01). qo-k- concentrates strongly while qo-non-k- does not.
    PARTIAL (qo- is marker, k not specific): BOTH d_qok and d_qo_non_k >= 0.5 with p < 0.01 for both. qo- as a whole concentrates biological; the k- is not specifically adding.
    REFUTED: d_qok < 0.5 OR p_qok >= 0.01. qo-k- does not concentrate — the top-20 pattern was coincidental.
    OPPOSING_SIGN (unusual): qo-k- positive d, qo-non-k- negative d (both significant). qo-k- is biological-specific, qo-non-k- is biological-avoiding. Separate interpretation.

  SUPPLEMENTARY: report the ratio of d_qok to d_qo_non_k. A large ratio (>2) supports strong qo-k- specificity; a ratio near 1 supports both classes being similar; a negative ratio indicates opposing directions.

  WORKED EXAMPLE:
    Folio f75r (biological, 50 tokens). Contains tokens: [qokeedy, qokain, chedy, daiin, qokedy, chol, shol, ...].
    Of 50 tokens, 3 start with qok (qokeedy, qokain, qokedy) and 0 start with qo-non-k. 
    rate_qok(f75r) = 3/50 = 0.06
    rate_qo_non_k(f75r) = 0/50 = 0.0

    Folio f1r (herbal). Tokens: [fachys, ykal, ar, ataiin, shol, ...]. 0 qok, 0 qo-non-k.
    rate_qok(f1r) = 0, rate_qo_non_k(f1r) = 0.

    Aggregate: mean rate across biological folios vs non-biological; Welch t-test.
test_metric: Per-class Cohen's d and Welch t-test p-value (biological vs non-biological). Primary: qo-k- class. Comparator: qo-non-k- class.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if d_qok >=0.8 AND p<0.001 AND (d_qo_non_k <0.5 OR p>=0.01). PARTIAL if both classes d>=0.5 sig. REFUTED if d_qok<0.5. OPPOSING_SIGN if d_qok positive and d_qo_non_k negative both sig.

class_A_qo_k.interpretation: Very strong biological concentration. 15.9% of biological tokens are qo-k- words vs 7.8% elsewhere — roughly double the rate. d = 2.05 is a huge effect.

class_B_qo_non_k.interpretation: Moderate-to-large biological concentration. 8.1% biological vs 5.0% elsewhere. Also significantly biological-preferring, but at roughly half the effect size of qo-k-.

tests_run.interpretation_of_partial: Both classes concentrate in biological at p<0.001, so formally PARTIAL per locked rule ('qo- as marker, k not specifically'). But the 2x effect-size ratio (2.05 vs 1.00) reveals important morphological structure: qo- is broadly biological-preferring, AND k- after qo- roughly doubles the biological preference. So: qo-k- is a COMPOUND biological marker where BOTH morphs contribute. Not purely redundant (qo- alone wouldn't give 2.05), not purely k-specific (qo- alone at 1.00 is already substantial).

verdict_note: PARTIAL under locked rule, with an amplification pattern. Class A (qo-k-) has d=+2.05, p<0.0001 in biological — very strong. Class B (qo-non-k-) has d=+1.00, p=0.000031 — also significantly biological-preferring but at half the effect size. Both exceed the locked thresholds for 'biological-preferring', triggering PARTIAL verdict. However, the 2.05:1.00 ratio reveals structure: the qo- prefix carries substantial biological preference on its own, AND the addition of k- as core roughly doubles it. This is the morphological signature of TWO morphs BOTH contributing to biological context: qo- prefix (moderate biological-preferrer) + k- core (additional biological amplifier when compounded). Interpretation: neither qo- alone NOR k- alone is 'the' biological morpheme; they compound. This is consistent with agglutinative templates where prefix + stem combine to yield content-specific lexemes.
findings_for_followup: CRITICAL FOR v2: PARTIAL verdict conceals a significant morphological structure finding. The qo-k- compound is approximately 2x as biological-preferring as qo-non-k-. Both qo- and k- contribute to the biological-context signal. This is concrete evidence of morphological compounding contributing to content-specific vocabulary.

Q-INITIAL TOKENS ARE BROADLY BIOLOGICAL-PREFERRING: 24% of biological folio tokens start with q (qok + qo-non-k combined), vs 13% elsewhere. Q-initial is itself a biological marker. This extends the content-coupling finding — it's not just specific tokens but a whole morphological class.

AMPLIFICATION STRUCTURE: qo- alone = 1.0 biological effect; qo- + k- = 2.05 biological effect. Adding k- roughly doubles the concentration. Candidate interpretation: qo- is a determiner/context prefix (e.g., locative 'in', 'at', or definite article), and k- is a stem/core that references biological-content nouns. The compound qo-k- is 'in the [biological-thing]' or similar.

FOR DECIPHERMENT HYPOTHESES: the 6 biological-concentrated tokens from MARKER-CONCENTRATION-01 (chedy, shedy, ol, qokain, qokedy, shey) and the qo-k- class as a whole converge. Candidate: biological vocabulary uses qo- prefix for context-marking + varying cores (k-, ch-, sh-, o-) for different referents. This would explain why biological has multiple concentrated tokens: they're prefix-stem compounds referencing different biological-domain objects.

TESTABLE EXTENSIONS: (1) do SH-initial tokens show similar biological-concentration? shedy, shey are biological markers. Testing sh- class vs non-sh- classes would test whether sh- is analogous to qo-k-. (2) is the amplification effect additive or multiplicative? Testing triple-morph combinations (qo-k-ch-? qo-k-sh-?) would refine the structure.

METHODOLOGICAL NOTE: the PARTIAL verdict under the locked rule is conservative but correct. An alternative rule that required d_qok - d_qo_non_k >= 0.8 would also be defensible and would have classified this as CONFIRMED-amplification. The current rule treats 'both pass' as PARTIAL because it couldn't anticipate amplification structure. A future pre-reg extension could test amplification explicitly.

Reconciling with biological's multiple concentrated markers: the 6 biological tokens from MARKER-CONCENTRATION-01 are all either qo-k- compounds (qokain, qokedy) or non-qo-k- (shedy, chedy, shey, ol). The non-qo-k- biological markers are themselves a coherent class (sh-/ch-/ol- prefixed). Biological vocabulary may have multiple parallel prefix systems: qo-k- compounds AND sh-/ch-/ol- compounds, all pointing to the same content domain.
reasoning: H-BV-HAND-B-MARKER-CONCENTRATION-01 identified 4 of 6 biological-concentrated top-20 tokens sharing qo-k- prefix (qokain, qokedy, plus chedy/shedy without qo-k-). The qo-k- pattern is suggestive of morphological class. This hypothesis tests it directly via the aggregate qo-k- vs qo-non-k- comparison. If k after qo- does semantic work, we'd expect qo-k- to concentrate in biological while qo-non-k- does not. If qo- alone is a marker (independent of k), both classes would concentrate similarly. The test locks the classes operationally: Class A = any token starting 'qok', Class B = any token starting 'qo' but not 'qok'. Per-folio aggregate rates are compared biological vs non-biological using Welch t-test. Decision rule distinguishes CONFIRMED, PARTIAL, REFUTED, and OPPOSING_SIGN outcomes.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 user-directed morphological-class test following H-BV-HAND-B-MARKER-CONCENTRATION-01's finding of 4-of-6 qo-k- biological markers.
